assistantPage.emptyBody: Documents, deadlines, EVMs, VVPAT, eligibility — try a suggested question or type your own.
assistantPage.historyLocal: History saves to your device only.

globalFaqs.q: Is VoteRoute affiliated with the Election Commission?
globalFaqs.a: No. VoteRoute is an independent guide that follows ECI-published procedures. For official forms and status, always use voters.eci.gov.in.
Everything stays on your device in localStorage. We never send your name, age, or location to a server.

globalFaqs.q: What is the difference between Voter ID and e-EPIC?
globalFaqs.a: They represent the same voter identity. e-EPIC is the digital PDF version you can download instantly, while the physical Voter ID card arrives later.

globalFaqs.q: Can I vote if I am in a different city on election day?
globalFaqs.a: Usually no — you must vote at your assigned booth. Plan ahead or check whether you qualify for a postal ballot.
Voter-Verifiable Paper Audit Trail — a printer attached to the EVM that briefly shows the candidate name and symbol after you vote.

s1.title: Let us check if you are eligible ✨
s1.shortDesc: A quick 30-second confirmation before we start.
s1.longDesc: To vote in India, you need to be an Indian citizen, be 18 or older on the qualifying date, and live in the constituency where you want to register.
s1.why: Checking eligibility first prevents wasted effort and helps us guide you correctly.
s1.consequence: If you skip this, you may complete a form that gets rejected later and lose valuable time.
checklist: I will be 18 or older on 1 January

faqs.q: I turn 18 next month — am I eligible?
faqs.a: Eligibility depends on the qualifying date. If you turn 18 after that date, you may be included in the next revision cycle.

faqs.q: I am an Indian citizen living abroad. Can I vote?
faqs.a: Yes. Overseas citizens can register as overseas electors and vote in person in their home constituency.

s2.title: Gather your 3 essentials 📂
s2.shortDesc: A photo, an age proof, and an address proof.
s2.longDesc: Keep clear photos or PDFs ready. Blurry uploads are one of the most common reasons applications get rejected.
s2.why: Having documents ready before opening the form saves time and reduces rejections.
s2.consequence: Missing or unclear documents can lead to rejection and force you to start again.
documents: Age proof — birth certificate, marksheet, passport, or PAN
Address proof — utility bill, rental agreement, bank statement, or Aadhaar

faqs.q: My address proof is in my parent’s name. Is that okay?
faqs.a: Usually yes, along with a supporting self-declaration if you live with family.

faqs.q: Can I use a selfie as my photo?
faqs.a: Use a passport-style photo with a plain background and clear lighting.

s3.title: Get officially registered 🪪
s3.shortDesc: Submit Form 6 on the official voter portal.
s3.longDesc: Open the official voter portal, create an account, and fill Form 6 with details exactly as shown on your documents.
s3.deadline: At least 30 days before election day
s3.why: Without Form 6 being filed and accepted, your name will not appear on the electoral roll.
s3.consequence: If you miss the cutoff, you may not be able to vote in the next election.

faqs.q: What if I make a typo in my name?
faqs.a: You can file a correction request afterward.

s4.title: Upload your documents correctly 📤
s4.shortDesc: Attach your photo, age proof, and address proof.
s4.longDesc: Keep each file within the allowed size and make sure every document is readable. Good lighting and visible corners help.
s4.why: Clear uploads make verification faster and reduce the chance of rejection.
s4.consequence: Unreadable files or wrong formats can delay or reject your application.

s5.title: Track your application 🔎
s5.shortDesc: Use your reference number to follow progress.
s5.longDesc: After submission, keep your reference ID and watch for updates. The Booth Level Officer may call or visit for verification.
s5.why: Tracking helps you react quickly if any follow-up is needed.
s5.consequence: If you miss verification follow-up, your application may be rejected silently.

s6.title: Your Voter ID is ready 🎟️
s6.longDesc: Once approved, you can download the digital version of your voter identity and keep it on your phone.
s6.why: The e-EPIC gives you immediate proof of voter registration.
s6.consequence: Without valid ID on polling day, you may be turned away.

s7.title: Find your polling booth 📍
s7.shortDesc: Locate your assigned station before election day.
s7.longDesc: Search using your voter ID or name and save the booth address before polling day.
s7.why: Knowing your booth in advance reduces stress and prevents last-minute confusion.
s7.consequence: Going to the wrong booth can stop you from voting that day.

s8.title: Cast your vote — you are ready 🗳️
s8.shortDesc: Bring your Voter ID and vote with confidence.
s8.longDesc: Reach your booth during polling hours, verify your identity, use the EVM, and confirm the VVPAT slip.
s8.why: This is the final and most important step of the journey.
s8.consequence: If you skip this step, the entire effort stays incomplete.

faqs.q: What if my name is not on the roll at the booth?
faqs.a: Ask the officer what options are available and whether you qualify for a tendered ballot.

faqs.q: Can I take my phone inside?
faqs.a: Phones and bags are generally not allowed inside the polling booth.
